data.title: Energy Transfer ticks dividend higher by 0.8% to $0.33,
data.description:
• Energy Transfer (NYSE:ET) declares $0.33/share quarterly dividend, 0.8% increase from prior dividend of $0.33.
  • Forward yield 7.45%
  • Payable Aug. 19; for shareholders of record Aug. 8; ex-div Aug. 8.
  • In addition, as previously announced, Energy Transfer plans to release earnings for the second quarter of 2025 on Wednesday, August 6, 2025, after the market closes.
  • See ET Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Ocean Power Technologies signs reseller agreement in Latin America
data.description:
• Ocean Power Technologies (NYSE:OPTT) on Thursday announced the signing of a new reseller agreement with a Latin America-based partner focused on supporting the Mexican offshore and maritime markets.
  • Under the agreement, the partner will provide business development, sales, and technical support services. The agreement also includes a $3 million purchase commitment for OPT’s WAM-V Unmanned Surface Vehicles.
  • OPTT +7.34% after hours to $0.81.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Orchid Island suffers loss in Q2 amid tumultuous markets
data.description:
Orchid Island Capital (NYSE:ORC) posted a Q2 loss as Liberation Day tariffs and the One Big, Beautiful Bill roiled markets. While many assets recovered, the agency RMBS sector did not.

Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.29, vs. the average analyst estimate of $0.17, swung from $0.18 in Q1 and -$0.09 in last year’s Q2.

Net interest income of $23.2M increased from $19.7M in the previous quarter and -$697K in the year-ago quarter.

Net realized and unrealized gains of $51.7M, or $0.45 per share, on RMBS and derivative instruments, climbed from $1.6M, or $0.02 per common share in Q1.

Book value of $7.21 per common share at June 30, 2025, compared with $7.94 at March 31, 2025.

Total return of -4.66% vs. 2.60% in the prior quarter.

Q2 expenses of $5.00M increased from $4.23M in Q1 and $4.38M in Q2 2024.

"The second quarter of 2025 was a very turbulent period for financial markets, with two large catalysts driving the volatility." said Chairman and CEO Robert E. Cauley, "The initial shock, and clearly the larger of the two, were reciprocal tariffs announced by the Trump administration in early April."

The second catalyst Congress passing the tax and spending bill, known as "The One Big, Beautiful Bill."

"While risk assets generally recovered from the severe turmoil early in the quarter, the agency RMBS sector did not fully recover, at least versus comparable duration hedges, resulting in negative excess returns for the quarter," Cauley said.

Orchid Island Capital (NYSE:ORC) stock fell 0.2% in Thursday after-hours trading.

Conference call on July 25 at 10:00 AM ET.

data.title: Stack Capital Group announces best efforts financing and concurrent private placement for up to $25M
data.description:
• Stack Capital Group (TSX:STCK:CA) on Thursday announced the terms of a "best efforts" private placement and concurrent non-brokered private placement for aggregate gross proceeds to the company of up to $25M.
  • In connection with the offering, the company announced an agreement in connection with a "best efforts" private placement of up to 727,272 units at a price of $13.75 per unit for gross proceeds of up to $10M (the "LIFE Offering").
  • In addition, the company also announces the terms of a concurrent non-brokered private placement of up to 1.09M units at the offering price to certain investors, for gross proceeds of up to $15M or such higher number as determined by the company in its discretion.
  • The net proceeds of the offering will be used for investments and general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about August 8.
  • At the closing of the offering, the company will pay to the agents a cash fee equal to 5.0% of the gross proceeds raised in connection with the offering.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Spruce Biosciences to enact 1-for-75 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Spruce Biosciences (OTC:SPRB) will implement a 1-for-75 reverse stock split effective August 4, 2025.

  • Post-split trading will begin on August 5, 2025, under the same ticker “SPRB”.

  • The reverse split aims to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s $1 minimum bid requirement.

  • Outstanding shares will shrink from ~42.2M to ~0.6M.

  • Source: Press release
